dialogueChunks.intro: Report just came in, {player_name}. The situation has changed drastically.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The target doubled back. While you were tracking {location_hint}, it circled around and hit {location} again. Took more {quest_item} and injured {target_npc} in the process. This thing is smarter than we thought: it's not running, it's playing with us. We found a second trail heading in a completely different direction, and {target_npc} swears they saw at least two more {enemy_type} with it this time.
dialogueChunks.outro: It's building a force. We need to find its base before this becomes a full-scale siege.

playerResponses.text: Smart prey makes for a better hunt. I'll find its den.
You said this was one target. Now it's a warband? I need more support.
This is out of control. You need an army, not one person.

dialogueChunks.intro: We've been played, {player_name}. That trail was a decoy.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The tracks you followed? Planted deliberately to lead us in circles while the real target moved {location_hint}. A farmer near the edge of {location} saw {enemy_type} dragging something heavy through the fields at midnight. Whatever they took, they're moving it toward the old ruins. {target_npc} thinks they're fortifying a position there, which means they're not planning to leave.
dialogueChunks.outro: We've lost time, but not the hunt. Get to those ruins before they dig in any deeper.

playerResponses.text: Decoys mean they're afraid of being found. Good. I'm close.
I wasted an entire day on a false trail. This better be the right one.
I'm chasing ghosts. Someone else can take over.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, listen carefully. This just became a rescue mission.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The {enemy_type} we've been tracking took a prisoner: {target_npc} was captured during their last raid on {location}. A runner spotted them being dragged toward a camp {location_hint}. If we go in with force, the captors might harm {target_npc} before we can reach them. But if we wait too long, they'll disappear with their hostage into the deep wilderness.
dialogueChunks.outro: We need speed and precision. Find the camp, locate {target_npc}, and bring them back alive. The {enemy_type} are secondary: the hostage is what matters.

playerResponses.text: I'll get them out. No one gets left behind.
Rescue and pursuit at once? This is going to cost extra.
A hostage situation is beyond what I signed up for.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, we've got a serious complication. The target isn't alone anymore.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The {enemy_type} you've been tracking linked up with a second group {location_hint}. What was a lone target is now a fortified warband with sentries, scouts, and a stockpile of stolen {quest_item}. {target_npc} watched them from a ridge and counted at least a dozen fighters. They're dug in and clearly not planning to move. Worse, they've posted lookouts on every approach.
dialogueChunks.outro: Brute force won't work against those numbers. You'll need to find a weakness in their defenses or draw them out somehow.

playerResponses.text: Numbers don't matter if their defenses have a gap. I'll find one.
A warband is not what I signed up for. Double the bounty or I walk.
A dozen fighters with fortifications? Send the army.

dialogueChunks.intro: Bad timing, {player_name}. The target just made things personal.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: They came back in the night and left a message nailed to the gate of {location}: a warning, written in blood, threatening to burn the settlement if we keep pursuing. {target_npc} found tracks heading {location_hint}, and alongside them, caches of {quest_item} arranged in a pattern. They're marking territory. This {enemy_type} isn't just running: it's claiming the land around us and daring us to respond.
dialogueChunks.outro: We can't ignore a direct threat like this. Find where they've dug in and show them that {location} doesn't back down.

playerResponses.text: Threats carved in blood? This ends now. Show me those tracks.
They've made it personal, and personal is expensive. Pay up.
Blood warnings and territory marking? This is above my pay grade.

dialogueChunks.intro: New intelligence, {player_name}. And none of it is good.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The {enemy_type} we've been chasing went underground: literally. {target_npc} tracked them to a tunnel entrance {location_hint} that connects to a network of old mining shafts beneath the hills. They've collapsed the entrance behind them, but there's a second access point deeper in the forest. The tunnels are narrow, dark, and perfect for an ambush. They've taken the {quest_item} down there with them, and they're using the underground network to move without being seen.
dialogueChunks.outro: Underground pursuit is the worst kind of hunt: tight quarters, no visibility, enemy advantage. But if we don't go in, they'll keep striking from below.

playerResponses.text: Tight quarters favor a single skilled fighter. I'll flush them out.
Underground in enemy territory. Just when I thought this couldn't get worse.
I don't fight in tunnels. Find me when they surface.

dialogueChunks.intro: I just got word from {target_npc}, {player_name}. The hunt just fractured.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The target split into three groups heading in different directions. One went {location_hint}, another doubled back toward {location}, and the third disappeared into the marshes. Each group is carrying some of the stolen {quest_item}, and we don't know which one has the leader. It's a classic scatter maneuver: force the pursuer to choose and hope they pick wrong. {target_npc} is watching the one heading toward town. You need to track the other two.
dialogueChunks.outro: Pick up the trails before they go cold. If even one group escapes, this whole pursuit was for nothing.

playerResponses.text: Two trails, two groups. I'll run them both down.
Splitting up is clever. But I've hunted clever prey before.
Three groups in three directions? That's three problems. I signed up for one.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, hold up. The target set a trap and one of our scouts walked right into it.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} was scouting the ridge {location_hint} when the {enemy_type} sprung an ambush. They're pinned down behind a rock outcropping with no way out. The {enemy_type} aren't pressing the attack: they're using {target_npc} as bait to lure in a rescue party. Smart. Ruthless. And effective, because we can't just leave {target_npc} there. The stolen {quest_item} is secondary right now: we need to extract our scout without walking into the same trap.
dialogueChunks.outro: Get {target_npc} out alive and then we finish this hunt. The {enemy_type} think they're the predators here. Time to correct that assumption.

playerResponses.text: They want me to walk into a trap? I'll spring it on my terms.
Rescue and counterattack at once. That's going to take everything I've got.
A baited trap with a hostage? This has gone too far.
